about.title: About TintMap
about.body:
Some obsessions start quietly. Mine started with a growing shelf of watercolor paints, too many brands to keep track of, and a nagging feeling that I had no idea what I actually owned.

I'm a hobbyist watercolor painter - not a professional artist, not a paint chemist, just someone who finds genuine joy in understanding pigments, pushing colors around wet paper, and falling down very deep rabbit holes about why Prussian Blue behaves so differently from Phthalo. Over time my collection grew to the point where managing it became its own challenge. Which White Nights paints are still in production? Did Winsor & Newton change the pigment in that particular green between editions? Which brands even publish a complete, historical list of their entire range in one place?

The honest answer is: most don't. And piecing it all together across PDFs, archived pages, and manufacturer websites was exactly the kind of meticulous, slightly maddening work that only someone truly enchanted by paint would actually do.

Why Not Just Use a Spreadsheet?
I did, for a while. But a spreadsheet lives on my laptop, helps only me, and can't be stumbled upon by another painter at 11pm wondering the same questions. If I was going to do the work of systematising pigment data, paint availability, colour index names, and brand histories, it felt worth building something shareable.

A Learning Project, Built with AI
Tintmap is also, frankly, an experiment. One of my goals was to learn how to use Codex - an AI coding tool - by actually building something real with it. That means this site has a genuinely work-in-progress quality to it. You may notice things shift, sections appear, or layouts update between visits. That's intentional growth, not neglect.

Because much of the data here is gathered from manufacturer websites using agentic (automated) processes, there may occasionally be brief periods where information is incomplete or slightly inaccurate before I've had a chance to review and correct it. I take accuracy seriously and always cross-reference against the source, but please extend some grace - this is one person, one passion project, and a learning curve happening in public.

What Tintmap Is (and Isn't)
This is not a business. There's no subscription, no affiliate angle, no grand commercial plan. It's a passion project from a painter who wanted to understand their collection better and thought others might benefit from the same clarity. The goal is for Tintmap to become a reliable reference - a place where a watercolor artist can look up a paint, understand its pigment makeup, trace how a colour has changed over years, and compare it across brands - all in one place.

We're not fully there yet. But every update gets us closer.

If you spot an error, have data to contribute, or just want to say hello - I'd genuinely love to hear from you. This is a community of curious painters, after all.

— Made with watercolor-stained hands and a lot of enthusiasm
